sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Occidental Petroleum (OXY) Price Target Trimmed by Analysts
feed.summary: Analysts have trimmed the price target for Occidental Petroleum Corporation (OXY) due to negative attention over the past few weeks, despite expectations for a stronger energy sector performance in 2026. UBS lowered its price target from $45 to $43, while BofA analyst Jean Ann Salisbury trimmed hers from $45 to $44, both maintaining a 'Neutral' rating on the shares. The article suggests focusing on AI stocks for better upside potential and less downside risk.

feed.title: Moderna Stock: Transition From Pandemic Windfall To Multi-Product Platform (NASDAQ:MRNA)
feed.summary: Moderna (MRNA) is initiated with a Hold rating and a $25 target price, lower than its current trading price, due to balanced risk-reward despite its advanced mRNA platform and robust pipeline. While near-term execution in respiratory vaccines is improving, COVID-19 demand remains unpredictable, and upcoming launches in RSV, flu, and combination vaccines face regulatory and competitive uncertainties. Setbacks in CMV and execution risks in rare disease and oncology temper long-term commercial optimism for the company.

feed.title: Cup Runneth Over: Chipotle Doubles Down on Protein
feed.summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill has launched a new "High Protein Menu" across the United States and Canada, featuring items ranging from 15 to 81 grams of protein. The menu includes the new High Protein Cup, inspired by customer social media posts, offering 32 grams of protein from Adobo chicken or steak. This initiative caters to the ongoing consumer demand for protein-rich and relatively affordable food options.

feed.title: Innoviz unveils next-gen LiDAR sensor for safer autonomous driving
feed.summary: Innoviz Technologies has launched InnovizThree, a smaller and lighter automotive-grade LiDAR sensor designed to accelerate autonomous driving adoption. This new sensor, weighing 600 grams and costing 35% less than its predecessor, offers high performance with reduced power consumption and strong long-range detection beyond 250 meters. Its compact design makes it suitable for various applications, including robotics and drones, and will be showcased at CES 2026.

feed.title: McDonald’s Corporation (MCD): Our Calculation of Intrinsic Value
feed.summary: This article presents a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis for McDonald's Corporation (MCD), calculating an intrinsic value of $95 per share. Despite the company's strong global brand, robust cash flows, and excellent operational durability, the current market price of around $319 suggests it is significantly overvalued by approximately 70% based on conservative assumptions. The analysis highlights McDonald's status as a high-quality consumer business but cautions against the limited margin of safety at its present valuation.

feed.title: Does FDA’s UPLIZNA gMG Expansion Reshape the Bull Case for Amgen’s Diversification Story (AMGN)?
feed.summary: The FDA's approval of Amgen's UPLIZNA for generalized myasthenia gravis, targeting AChR- and MuSK-antibody positive adults, significantly boosts the company's autoimmune portfolio. This expansion supports Amgen's strategy to diversify revenue beyond its established bone-health and oncology franchises, reinforcing its investment narrative amidst ongoing biosimilar and pricing pressures. While the approval strengthens Amgen's pipeline breadth, its long-term impact will depend on how effectively it offsets challenges from maturing legacy products and regulatory scrutiny on drug prices.

feed.title: Sands China units renew to end of 2028 trademark and marketing services deals with Las Vegas Sands parent
feed.summary: Sands China Ltd. has renewed trademark and marketing services agreements with its U.S. parent, Las Vegas Sands Corp., until December 31, 2028. Four Macau-registered Sands China subsidiaries will pay an annual royalty of 1.5 percent of gross non-gaming and gaming revenue for trademark use, with annual caps set for 2026, 2027, and 2028. The renewals are crucial for the marketing and corporate identity of Sands China's businesses in Macau.

feed.title: Branding deal for MGM China to double percentage of adjusted consolidated net monthly revenues payable for MGM brand use
feed.summary: MGM Resorts International, the majority owner of MGM China Holdings Ltd, has announced a new "long-term branding agreement" which will double the licensing fee MGM China pays for using the 'MGM' brand, from 1.75 percent to 3.5 percent of adjusted consolidated net monthly revenues. This new agreement, effective January 1, 2026, will run until 2032 and is subject to an annual cap, estimated at US$188 million for full-year 2026, with MGM Resorts retaining 67% and Pansy Ho Chiu King retaining the balance. The deal is meant to secure MGM China's "most important intangible asset" and provides MGM Resorts with "fair compensation" for brand use.

feed.title: Japan Livestock Health and Nutrition Focus Drives Growth in the Animal Feed Organic Trace Minerals Market | Top Companies - Alltech Inc, Royal DSM, Archer Daniels Midland Company
feed.summary: The global Animal Feed Organic Trace Minerals Market is projected to grow at a high CAGR from 2023-2030, driven by the increasing focus on livestock health and productivity. The report highlights Japan's recent industry developments, such as Ajinomoto Animal Nutrition's organic selenium launch and Zen-Noh's inclusion of organic zinc in feed, alongside similar advancements in the United States. Key market players include Alltech, Royal DSM, and Archer Daniels Midland Company, with zinc, dry forms, and dairy cattle/poultry applications dominating the market segments.

feed.title: Here is Why Dominion Energy (D) Fell This Week
feed.summary: Dominion Energy (D) shares fell 1.83% this week, largely due to the Trump administration suspending leases for five offshore wind projects, including Dominion's major Coastal Virginia Offshore Wind project, citing national security concerns. This 90-day halt further delays a decade-long project. Additionally, Morgan Stanley analyst David Arco lowered Dominion's price target from $65 to $62 while maintaining an 'Equal Weight' rating.
